purpose: Documents Synapse's two complementary preservation systems for swarm data: archives (full directory snapshots in Archive/{YYYY-MM-DD}_{task_name}/) and history (lightweight JSON summaries in history/{YYYY-MM-DD}_{task_name}.json). Specifies the non-negotiable archive-before-clear rule that runs whenever a dashboard is reset, and the !reset command flow including --all variant.
gotchas: Archive-before-clear is non-negotiable -- previous swarm data is NEVER discarded, only archived
Archive date is the clear date, not the swarm start date
If task.name is missing the archive falls back to 'unnamed' as the slug
!reset extends history with a lessons_learned object (deviations, failure_causes, warnings, patterns)
patterns: Snapshot + summary dual preservation (full data + quick browsing)
Pre-clear archive ensures zero data loss across dashboard reuse
conventions: Archive naming: {YYYY-MM-DD}_{task_name}/ directory; history: same name with .json suffix
ArchiveService and HistoryService are the canonical writers
